title: Programming Fundamentals
learning_outcomes: Read and understand coding solutions.
Apply basic data structures, such as arrays and linked lists, to solve complex problems.
Apply C programming language to solve simple decision, looping, array, and linked list problems programmatically.
Review the produced code against specification criteria by applying testing techniques.

title: Computer Systems Fundamentals
learning_outcomes: Design, implement, and analyse small programs at the assembly/machine level.
Describe the components comprising, and the services offered by, an operating system.
Explain how the major components of a CPU work together, including how data and instructions are represented in a computer.
Implement simple programs involving communication and concurrency.

Describe the relationship between a high-level procedural language (C) and assembly (machine language) which implements it, including how a compiled program is executed in a classical von Neumann machine.
Describe the architectural layers (fundamental parts) of a modern computer system from hardware device (chip) levels upwards.

title: Computing for Engineers
learning_outcomes: Implement algorithms using the Python programming language.
Write Python programs to automate many different tasks.
Use, at a simple level, the numerical computation packages of Matlab and Excel.
Design algorithms to solve computation problems.

title: Mathematics 1A
learning_outcomes: Use technology as an aid to solve appropriate problems in Algebra and Calculus.
Apply concepts in Algebra and Calculus to unexpected contexts.
Communicate mathematical ideas in written form using correct terminology and using technology.
Apply concepts and techniques from Algebra and Calculus to solve problems.
Identify and construct valid mathematical arguments.
Apply definitions and theorems in Algebra and Calculus to justify mathematical statements and solve problems.

title: Higher Mathematics 1A
learning_outcomes: Communicate mathematical ideas in written and oral form using correct terminology and using technology.
Use technology as an aid to solve appropriate problems in Algebra and Calculus.
Apply concepts in Algebra and Calculus to unexpected contexts.
Identify and construct valid mathematical arguments and proofs of theorems.
Apply concepts and techniques from Algebra and Calculus to solve problems.
Apply definitions and theorems in Algebra and Calculus to justify mathematical statements and solve problems.

title: Mathematics 1B
learning_outcomes: Apply concepts in Algebra and Calculus to unexpected contexts.
Apply concepts and techniques from Algebra and Calculus to solve problems.
Apply definitions and theorems in Algebra and Calculus to justify mathematical statements and solve problems.
Communicate mathematical ideas in written and oral form using correct terminology and using technology.
Identify and construct valid mathematical arguments in the context of Algebra and Calculus.
Use technology as an aid to solve appropriate problems in Algebra and Calculus.

title: Higher Mathematics 1B
learning_outcomes: Communicate mathematical ideas in written and oral form using correct terminology and using technology.
Apply concepts and techniques from Algebra and Calculus to solve problems.
Apply concepts in Algebra and Calculus to unexpected contexts.
Identify and construct valid mathematical arguments and proofs of theorems in the context of Algebra and Calculus.
Apply definitions and theorems in Algebra and Calculus to justify mathematical statements and solve problems.
Use technology as an aid to solve appropriate problems in Algebra and Calculus.

title: Microeconomics 1
learning_outcomes: Articulate how individuals and society as a whole benefit or are harmed by economic markets.
Evaluate the costs and benefits of government policies that affect economic incentives and behaviour.
Explain the assumptions that underlie standard microeconomic models of individual and collective decision-making and behaviour in a world with limited resources.
Use basic mathematical tools to demonstrate the properties and predictions of economic models and calculate the effect of changes in economic variables.

Identify appropriate economic models (e.g., models of perfectly competitive markets and various market imperfections) and apply them to analyse and predict the behaviour of individuals and firms interacting in markets.
